units.title: Feelings & Emotions
units.subtitle: Happy, Sad, Angry, Sleepy, Hungry, Thirsty
grammar_focus: Yes, I am / No, I am not.

lessons.title: How Are You?
lessons.topic: Basic emotions and How are you?

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: How does the boy feel when he hears the birds?
What does the boy do when he's happy?
questions.hint: It's something you do with your mouth!

questions.question_text: What makes the boy feel better when he is sad?
questions.hint: It's something you do with others!

items.example: How are you? I am happy.
Are you sad? No, I am not.

steps.title: Amy and Tom's Day
steps.text: Hello, Amy! How do you feel? I am happy. How are you, Tom? I am sad. Where is my teddy bear?

steps.explanation: Use this to talk about how you feel.
examples: How are you? I am excited.
How are you? I am sleepy.
How are you? I am okay.

items.sentence: How am you? I am happy.
How are you? I is sad.

steps.audio_text: Hello! How do you feel today? I am happy because the sun is shining.

questions.question_text: I ___ happy. (Grammar question)
What does 'sad' mean? (Vocabulary question)
True or False: 'How are you?' is asking about feelings.
Which sentence is correct? (Reading comprehension question)

lessons.title: I Want Water
lessons.topic: Physical needs and Are you...? questions

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: How do you feel when you don't have enough water?
What do we need to drink to stay healthy?
Where do kids have fun swinging and sliding?

items.example: Are you hungry? Yes, I am.
How are you? I am thirsty.
Do you want to eat? Yes, I do.
Are you thirsty? Yes, I will drink.

steps.title: Park Adventure
steps.text: How are you? I am hungry. Are you thirsty? Yes, I am. Let's eat a sandwich and drink some juice. Are you happy now? Yes, I am!

steps.explanation: Use this to talk about feelings.
examples: Are you sleepy? Yes, I am.
Are you happy? Yes, I am.
Are you cold? No, I am not.

items.sentence: Are you thirsty? No, I ___ not.
Are you are happy? Yes, I am.

steps.audio_text: Hi Lucy, how are you feeling? I'm a little thirsty. Can we have some juice?

questions.question: What does Lucy want to drink?

questions.question_text: Are you ___? (Grammar question)
What do you drink when you are thirsty? (Vocabulary question)
The boy asks, 'Are you hungry?' She says 'No, I am not.' (Reading question)
Fill in the blank: 'Are you ___?' 'Yes, I __.'
Choose what you need if you want water. (Comprehension question)

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What does the little boy feel after playing all day?
questions.hint: You need to sleep when you feel this way.

questions.question_text: What do you feel when you eat your favorite food?
questions.hint: It makes you smile and feel good inside.

questions.question_text: What do you do when you feel hungry?
questions.hint: Your tummy wants this to stop rumbling.

items.example: Are you angry? Yes, I am.
How are you? I am sleepy.
Are you mad? No, I am not.
It is night. I am sleepy.

steps.title: Sleepy Night
steps.text: It is night. I am sleepy. My dog is sleepy too. Are you mad? No, I am not. I am happy.

steps.explanation: Ask and answer how you feel.
examples: Are you sleepy? Yes, I am.
Are you angry? No, I am not.
Are you happy? Yes, I am.

items.sentence: My face is ___. I am angry.
My face is ___. I am happy.
My face is red. I am feel angry.
My face are yellow. I am happy.

steps.audio_text: Maria, are you mad? No, I am not mad. I am just sleepy because it's already night.

steps.prompt: Say: My face is red. I am mad.
steps.expected_text: my face is red. i am mad.

questions.question_text: My face is [____]. I am angry. (Grammar question)
What does 'sleepy' mean? (Vocabulary question)
Is it time to sleep at night? (Comprehension question)
Which feeling is the opposite of 'angry'? (Vocabulary question)
When you say 'I am mad', what does 'mad' mean? (Reading comprehension question)

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What is the best drink when you are very thirsty?
questions.hint: It's clear and helps the most.

questions.question_text: What do you feel after running a lot on a sunny day?
questions.hint: Your mouth feels dry and you really need a drink.

questions.question_text: If you want to cool down quickly, what should you have?
questions.hint: It's something cold and refreshing that you drink.

steps.title: A Happy Day
steps.text: Hi, I am Lucy. How are you? I am happy because I am playing. Are you happy too? Yes, I am!

steps.audio_text: Hello! How are you feeling today? I'm feeling happy because I'm going to see my friends. Do you like playing with friends?

steps.prompt: Say: How are you feeling now and why?
steps.expected_text: i am happy because i am playing

questions.question_text: I ___ happy today. (Grammar question)
Which emoji shows 'happy'? (Vocabulary question)
True or False: 'Are you sad?' asks about feelings.
What do you say if you feel good? (Reading comprehension question)
